prompt_md: A team must show inventory counts that change roughly once every few minutes, on a page that thousands of users keep open in background tabs. There are no latency-sensitive decisions made from the number. They're tempted to push every change over a persistent connection to each open page. What should they do, and why?

decision_choices: Push every change over a persistent connection to all open pages, since the data is technically live
Have clients poll for the current count at a sane interval instead of holding thousands of persistent connections open
Open a persistent connection but never handle reconnects, to keep the implementation small
Stop showing the count at all to avoid the problem entirely
reason_choices: Because updates are infrequent and non-urgent, so polling is simpler, cacheable, and avoids pinning thousands of idle connections — push's cost isn't justified here
Because persistent connections are always cheaper than periodic requests
Because polling makes the inventory number update faster than push ever could
Because real-time push is the only correct way to display any changing value
